sections.name: Detection order
rules: First determine whether the drawing has a BOM, parts list, item table, balloon numbers, leader lines, arrows, exploded views, detail views, or flat patterns.
If a BOM or parts list exists, bind each quote row by part number first, then by normalized name/specification, then by material and quantity.
A quote row number is not the same as a BOM item number. Always read the BOM item number from the parts list.
If a child BOM item also has a standalone detail drawing in the upload, keep the child row in the assembly hierarchy and use the standalone drawing as stronger visual evidence for that child.

sections.name: BOM to callout linking
rules: For a bound BOM item, search the drawing for the matching numbered callout bubble.
Follow the leader line from the bubble boundary to its endpoint or arrowhead.
The image target is the physical object under the arrowhead or leader endpoint, not the bubble and not the leader line itself.
If nearby leader lines cross, keep the original bubble identity and do not switch to a neighboring item number.
When the same item can be located both in an assembly callout and in a standalone detail drawing, prefer the smallest standalone detail view if it shows the specific part more clearly.

sections.name: Fallback order
rules: If no arrow target is visible, use a local detail view that isolates the same item.
If no graphic evidence exists, crop the exact BOM row as evidence.
If only notes or title blocks exist, return bbox null with low confidence.
